page: Amazon Web Services c7i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor operating at 3.2 GHz, delivering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes an L3 cache of 105 MB, alongside L1d and L2 caches of 48 KB and 2 MB respectively. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across several compute-intensive workloads. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in Geekbench multi-core tests and software compilation using Clang. It also shows strong performance in cryptographic and extended instruction execution, as evidenced by high Passmark CPU and encryption test scores. Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 3865.15 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 203.15 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, though write bandwidth for large blocks remains in the average tier at 81.54 GB/sec.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations. These hardware features translate to strong performance in medium-sized LLM inference tasks, achieving 196.44 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 28.84 tokens/sec for text generation on a 7B model. However, for larger 70B models, performance drops to average levels. The instance is highly suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compiling, ray tracing, high-throughput static web serving, and database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 96.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 19 Gbps baseline network interface. It excels in compute-heavy benchmarks, showing strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and cryptographic operations. Supported by Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, it delivers strong performance for medium-scale LLM inference and database operations. This instance is optimized for compute-bound workloads, including high-throughput web serving, scientific simulations, and CI/CD pipelines, offering high resource density for CPU-intensive tasks without the overhead of local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-performance CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory optimized for intensive parallel processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
96.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction support
Strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Best suited for compute-intensive workloads, CI/CD pipelines, and medium LLM inference
